name: AlphaX POS Card Reader

fields.label: Reader Name
fields.description: A short label, e.g. 'Geidea Counter 1' or 'Network Intl Booth A'. Used as the unique key.

fields.label: Vendor
fields.options:
Geidea
Network International
PayTabs
Tap
Checkout.com
Other

fields.label: Transport
fields.options:
MQTT
HTTP Webhook
Serial / USB
Direct TCP
fields.description: How the cashier talks to the reader. MQTT is most common for Geidea-style Android-app terminals; HTTP for cloud-based reader APIs; Serial/USB for legacy countertop readers; Direct TCP for a few high-end vendors.

fields.label: Terminal & User Mapping
fields.description: Which AlphaX terminal and/or user is this reader associated with. Either field can be set; both together makes the mapping more specific.

fields.label: User
fields.description: Optional: bind this reader to a specific user. Useful for mobile cashiers.

fields.label: Device Topic
fields.description: The MQTT topic the card reader subscribes to. Each reader has its own unique topic.

fields.label: HTTP Endpoint URL
fields.description: Card reader's REST endpoint to POST transactions to.

fields.label: Serial Port
fields.description: e.g. COM3 on Windows, /dev/ttyUSB0 on Linux. Handled by the AlphaX bridge daemon (not Frappe directly).

fields.label: Merchant ID (MID)
fields.description: Assigned by the acquiring bank to the merchant.

fields.label: Terminal ID (TID)
fields.description: Assigned by the acquiring bank to this specific reader. Different from the AlphaX terminal ID.

fields.label: Timeout (seconds)
fields.description: How long to wait for the cardholder to tap/insert/swipe before timing out the transaction.

fields.label: Extra Config (JSON)
fields.description: Free-form vendor-specific settings. Adapter code reads keys from here as needed (e.g. {"print_receipt_on_terminal": true}).

description: Configuration for a payment card reader attached to a POS terminal. Vendor-agnostic: supports MQTT (Geidea Android-app terminals), HTTP (cloud-based reader APIs), Serial/USB (countertop readers via bridge), and Direct TCP. Adapter code in alphax_pos_suite.integrations.card_reader picks the right transport based on this record.
